rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES KAVANAGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The decedent and petitioner signed the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) providing for the decedents child/spousal support obligations and division of property. The MOA provides that: This Memorandum contains all the essential terms of settlement and shall be independently enforceable under CCP section 664.6; this Memorandum is not protected by the mediation or settlement conference privilege. However, the parties intend and agree that a Marital Settlement Agreement (MSA) containing these and other standard terms will be prepared and signed by them. Probate Code section 145 provides in relevant part that a complete property settlement entered into after or in anticipation of separate or dissolution of marriage is a waiver by the spouse of the rights described in section 141(a). Does the MOA qualify as a complete property settlement contemplated by section 145? If so, is the petitioner spouse, a party to the MOA, deemed to have waived her right to receive any part of this estate by intestate succession under section 141(a)(1)? See Welch v. Welch (2022) 79 Cal. App. 5th 283. 2) The petitioner characterized 30.8929% of the family residence (86 San Jacinto Way, SF) as the decedent's separate property and 34.5535 x 2 = 69.1071% as community property. Section 7b of the MOA provides that: "Upon the sale of the house, after payment of costs of sale and any encumbrances on the house, the first $1,460,000 shall be distributed to Jim (decedent) to reimburse his separate property contribution and the remainder divided equally." Even if the family residence was acquired as community property, did the MOA operate as an agreement of spouses altering the community property characterization to separate property of each spouse in equal shares? See Henn v. Henn (1980) 264 Cal.3d 323, 330 [Under California law, a spouses entitlement to a share of the community property arises at the time the property is acquired. That interest is not altered except by judicial decree or an agreement between the parties.] 3) The accounting as filed does not balance. It should also be noted that the correct amount of statutory compensation is $156,068.47 based on the fee base of $18,613,694. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROY ELLIS KUTZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proposed distribution was AMENDED by the "Supplement" filed on 9/5/23. As noted in the prior tentative ruling, an amendment was required to be filed and notice of the amendment was required to be served after the amendment was filed. (2) Why is the decedent's ½ separate property interest in the real properties going entirely to Marguerite, not ½ to Marguerite and ½ to Elaine? Did Elaine assign her ½ interest to Marguerite? (3) A Corrected I&A should have been filed to reflect that the 5 shares of Kyndryl Holding, 13.596938 shares of Corteva, 12.719290 shares of The Chemoures Company, and 29.108560 shares of Dow were NOT assets on hand as of the date of death. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 11/13/23. An amending pleading requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A petition is either supplemented to provide additional information or amended to correct information. [CRC 7.3.] Examiner's Notes are not filed for the record and should not be "responded" to. Each filed pleading should stand on its own and not refer to Examiner's Notes.)

[LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) No proof of service has been filed. (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (5) No proof of publication of the "Corrected" petition appears to have been filed. Deadline is 11/13/23 for delivering the proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and all related papers to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
